title: Nashville 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Nashville 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Nashville, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum and Ryman Auditorium. Prices from $1969 per person (estimate).
highlights: Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Nashville, United States — Humid subtropical climate with mild winters (highs around 9-10°C, occasional cold snaps) and hot, humid summers (highs 31-32°C); spring brings the highest risk of thunderstorms. April to June and September to October bring mild temperatures, blooming or turning foliage and manageable humidity for walking downtown. This itinerary blends Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum, Ryman Auditorium, Lower Broadway honky-tonks with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum, Ryman Auditorium, Lower Broadway honky-tonks
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Nashville
itinerary.description: Land at Nashville International Airport (BNA) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum — Definitive collection of country music history and memorabilia. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum

itinerary.title: Nashville: Ryman Auditorium & Grand Ole Opry House show
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Ryman Auditorium — "Mother Church of Country Music," former Grand Ole Opry home. In the afternoon, continue to Grand Ole Opry House show — Live weekly country music radio show and concert. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Nashville: Johnny Cash Museum & The Parthenon
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Johnny Cash Museum — Museum dedicated to Johnny Cash's life and career. In the afternoon, continue to The Parthenon — Full-scale replica Greek Parthenon with a Athena statue, in Centennial Park. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Jack Daniel's Distillery, Lynchburg
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Jack Daniel's Distillery, Lynchburg, a popular side trip from Nashville. Return to Nashville in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Jack Daniel's Distillery, Lynchburg

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Fisk University's Jubilee Hall — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Nashville: Belle Meade Historic Site & RCA Studio B tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Belle Meade Historic Site — Antebellum plantation mansion and winery estate. In the afternoon, continue to RCA Studio B tour — Historic recording studio where Elvis and others cut hits. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Franklin historic district
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Franklin historic district, a popular side trip from Nashville. Return to Nashville in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Franklin historic district

itinerary.title: Nashville: Musicians Hall of Fame and Museum & Adventure Science Center
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Musicians Hall of Fame and Museum — Museum honoring session musicians across genres. In the afternoon, continue to Adventure Science Center — Hands-on science museum with a planetarium. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Musicians Hall of Fame and Museum

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Marathon Village distillery and shops — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to The Hermitage (Andrew Jackson's home)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to The Hermitage (Andrew Jackson's home), a popular side trip from Nashville. Return to Nashville in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: The Hermitage (Andrew Jackson's home)

itinerary.title: Nashville: Nashville Zoo & Lane Motor Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Nashville Zoo — Zoo with an award-winning Andean bear exhibit. In the afternoon, continue to Lane Motor Museum — Quirky collection of rare European and microcars. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Nashville: Lower Broadway honky-tonks & The Parthenon grounds and Centennial Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Lower Broadway honky-tonks — Strip of live-music bars, mostly free entry with tip jars. In the afternoon, continue to The Parthenon grounds and Centennial Park — Park setting for Nashville's famous Parthenon replica. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: The Parthenon grounds and Centennial Park

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Shelby Bottoms Greenway trails — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Music Row or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to BNA for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Grand Ole Opry and Ryman Auditorium tickets online well ahead — popular shows sell out.
Visit Lower Broadway honky-tonks earlier in the evening to avoid the loudest bachelorette-party crush.
A downtown-to-12-South or Gulch rideshare is often faster and cheaper than trying to find parking.
Try hot chicken at your own risk starting at "mild" — Nashville hot spice levels get genuinely intense.
CMA Fest in June sells out hotels citywide months ahead — book early if visiting that week.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Comfortable boots for honky-tonk hopping and uneven sidewalks
Light, breathable layers for humid summer evenings
A light jacket for cool winter mornings
Earplugs if sensitive to loud live music venues
thingsToDo: Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum
Musicians Hall of Fame and Museum
The Parthenon grounds and Centennial Park
thingsToAvoid: Overpaying for taxi rides from the airport — rideshare or the RTA Music City Star are cheaper.
Wandering into East Nashville or South Nashville side streets alone late at night.
Assuming every honky-tonk is free — some charge a cover for headline acts on weekend nights.
Driving downtown during CMA Fest or a Titans/Predators game — street closures cause gridlock.
Skipping sunscreen and water on a July afternoon walking Broadway — heat and humidity add up fast.
topRestaurants: Prince's Hot Chicken Shack — Nashville Hot Chicken ($)
Hattie B's Hot Chicken — Nashville Hot Chicken ($$)
The Loveless Cafe — Southern Comfort Food ($$)
The Catbird Seat — Tasting Menu Fine Dining ($$$$)
instagramSpots: Lower Broadway neon signs at night
Pedestrian Bridge over the Cumberland River
luxuryExperiences: Private songwriter round in a Music Row studio
Suite stay at The Hermitage Hotel
VIP box seats at the Grand Ole Opry
Bourbon and whiskey tasting flight downtown
honeymoonExperiences: Live music date night at the Ryman Auditorium
Riverside dinner cruise on the General Jackson Showboat
Couples spa day at a Gulch boutique hotel
Sunset stroll on the John Seigenthaler Pedestrian Bridge
budgetExperiences: Free live music on Lower Broadway (tip the band instead of a cover)
Wander the Parthenon grounds at Centennial Park
Nashville hot chicken at Prince's or Bolton's for a few dollars
Free walk through Germantown's historic streets
localEtiquette: Southern hospitality is genuine — expect "y'all," "sir/ma'am," and friendly small talk from strangers.
Tipping 18-20% at restaurants and bars is standard, and tipping bar bands playing on Broadway is expected.
Bachelorette parties dominate Lower Broadway on weekends; visitors joining honky-tonks should expect a boisterous scene.
Cowboy boots and hats are everyday wear here, not costume — dress as casual or as western as you like.
Church on Sunday still shapes local rhythm; some smaller businesses close or open late on Sunday mornings.
localTransportGuide: Nashville is car-dependent outside downtown — the WeGo bus network and a limited downtown trolley exist, but rideshare or a rental car is the practical way to reach 12 South, The Gulch and day-trip sites. Broadway, the Gulch and downtown core are very walkable for a night out.
bestTimeToVisit: April to June and September to October bring mild temperatures, blooming or turning foliage and manageable humidity for walking downtown.
weather: Humid subtropical climate with mild winters (highs around 9-10°C, occasional cold snaps) and hot, humid summers (highs 31-32°C); spring brings the highest risk of thunderstorms.
visaInformation: Visa-free ESTA entry for UK, EU, Australian and other Visa Waiver Program passport holders for stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a US B1/B2 visitor visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~14.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Nashville?
faqs.answer: Visa-free ESTA entry for UK, EU, Australian and other Visa Waiver Program passport holders for stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a US B1/B2 visitor visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Nashville?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October bring mild temperatures, blooming or turning foliage and manageable humidity for walking downtown. Humid subtropical climate with mild winters (highs around 9-10°C, occasional cold snaps) and hot, humid summers (highs 31-32°C); spring brings the highest risk of thunderstorms.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Nashville safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Nashville is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Nashville?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Wei Johnson
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. RCA Studio B tour in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Lucas Cohen
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Lower Broadway honky-tonks and Belle Meade Historic Site. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Sarah Cohen
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Nashville is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Music Row. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Wei Khan
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Fisk University campus in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Marco Fernandez
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Lower Broadway honky-tonks and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
